title: From Critique to Construction: Priorities for Signed-Language Writing Infrastructure
subtitle: A constructive synthesis for research, tooling, standards, and adoption
summary: A forward-looking synthesis that turns critique toward evidence, tools, corpora, interoperability, data governance, and stewardship.
synopsis: The critical review series points toward constructive priorities rather than diagnosis alone. The next work of the field belongs around evidence, corpora, tooling, interoperability, standards, education, data governance, stewardship, and collaboration, while keeping SignWriting's current infrastructural strengths visible without treating the field's open questions as settled.

slides.title: Point Of Construction
slides.summary: The review series should lead from diagnosis toward infrastructure priorities for signed-language writing.
slides.speech: This synthesis turns the review series from diagnosis toward construction. The goal is not only to say where prior work falls short, but to name practical priorities for evidence, corpora, tooling, standards, education, and collaboration.

image_prompts.concrete: Synthesis slide moving from critical diagnosis to practical infrastructure priorities for signed-language writing
image_prompts.abstract: A review path turning from critique toward construction

slides.title: What Review Clarified
slides.summary: The review series clarified that signed writing must be judged as writing and that durable text is the infrastructure threshold.
bullets: SignWriting has the strongest documented full path
slides.speech: The review series clarifies several things. Signed writing should be judged as writing, not only notation. Durable text is the real infrastructure threshold. SignWriting currently provides the strongest documented full path, while alternative systems still matter.
slides.notes: This slide summarizes the series without triumphalism.

image_prompts.concrete: Review synthesis slide showing durable text, writing as writing, SignWriting full-path strengths, and alternative systems as separate clarified findings
image_prompts.abstract: Lessons from review arranged into a constructive map

slides.title: Construction Priorities
slides.summary: The next priorities are evidence, corpora, tooling, interoperability, data governance, stewardship, education, handwriting, standards, and collaboration.
slides.speech: The constructive priorities are practical: evidence beyond plausibility, corpora and reusable signed text, tooling that lowers the cost of writing, interoperability, data governance, stewardship, education, handwriting and fluency, standards engagement, and broader collaboration.
slides.notes: Keep this as a usable agenda.

image_prompts.concrete: Agenda slide listing evidence, corpora, tooling, interoperability, data governance, stewardship, education, handwriting, standards, and collaboration as construction priorities
image_prompts.abstract: Practical infrastructure priorities radiating from durable signed text

slides.title: Constructive Tone
slides.summary: Construction should sound disciplined, collaborative, and evidence-building rather than dismissive or prematurely settled.
slides.speech: The final message is about tone and direction. Construction should sound disciplined, collaborative, evidence-building, and infrastructure-focused. The field's open questions are empirical and infrastructural, not reasons to stop.
slides.notes: Close the review series with momentum.

image_prompts.concrete: Closing synthesis slide showing disciplined, collaborative, evidence-building, infrastructure-focused work as the review series outcome
image_prompts.abstract: A careful critical tone becoming constructive momentum
